featureName: BUG-069-003: the direct POST /api/capture handler (internal/api/capture.go::CaptureHandler) binds the durable pipeline.Process write to the request context (r.Context()), so a client disconnect after the body is received but before the durable Postgres INSERT + NATS publish complete cancels the write and silently loses the capture — the SAME root cause as the already-fixed BUG-069-002 but at the direct /api/capture endpoint, which was never in BUG-069-002's scope. Violates the inviolable capture-as-fallback contract (Hard Constraint 5 / BS-001)

fixSequence.task: Author internal/api/capture_disconnect_test.go (TestCaptureHandler_CaptureSurvivesClientDisconnect) — RED against d.Pipeline.Process(r.Context(), ...). Then apply the one-line fix in internal/api/capture.go::CaptureHandler: d.Pipeline.Process(context.WithoutCancel(r.Context()), ...) and ADD the "context" import (capture.go did not import context). GREEN. Boundary: only capture.go (import + the durable Process call line + explanatory comment) + the new test file; response-path d.DB.Healthy(r.Context()) / writeJSON / writeError stay request-scoped. COMPLETE: RED+GREEN captured in report.md [R1][R3]; fix diff [R2]; handler-family regression [R4].

Author ONE SHARED live-stack E2E / integration regression that covers BOTH disconnect-durability surfaces in a single test: drive a real HTTP request whose client connection is aborted mid-write against (a) POST /api/assistant/turn (BUG-069-002) AND (b) POST /api/capture (this bug, BUG-069-003), each backed by a real pipeline.Processor (Postgres + NATS), asserting the captured artifact is persisted despite the disconnect on BOTH endpoints. This consolidates BUG-069-002 fixSequence order-2 and BUG-069-003 fixSequence order-2 into one regression (no duplicate live-stack harness). Requires `./smackerel.sh up` + `./smackerel.sh test integration|e2e`. Then route to bubbles.validate for done-ceiling certification (scenario-specific + broader E2E regression + stress) for BOTH bugs. NOT runnable in the discovery/fix session (no live stack).

completedPhaseClaimDetails.summary: MVP/evo-x2 readiness sweep finding F-01. Read the direct capture path: internal/api/capture.go::CaptureHandler dispatches d.Pipeline.Process(r.Context(), &pipeline.ProcessRequest{...}); pipeline.Processor.Process -> submitForProcessing runs a ctx-honoring Postgres INSERT (storeInitialArtifact) + NATS publish (NATS.Publish(ctx, ...)) (internal/pipeline/processor.go ~L471). net/http cancels r.Context() the instant the client connection drops, so a capture client (chrome extension / PWA / API caller) that disconnects after the body is received but before the durable write completes aborts Process with context.Canceled and the capture is SILENTLY LOST. Finding F-069-CR-CAPTURE-ENDPOINT-CTX-CANCEL (MEDIUM): same inviolable capture-as-fallback violation (Hard Constraint 5 / BS-001) as BUG-069-002, but at the DIRECT /api/capture endpoint which was never in BUG-069-002's scope (BUG-069-002 only fixed internal/assistant/httpadapter/adapter.go::ServeHTTP). Telegram unaffected (long-poll background ctx).

Scenario-first: authored internal/api/capture_disconnect_test.go (TestCaptureHandler_CaptureSurvivesClientDisconnect) reusing the established internal/api harness (mockDB/mockNATS from health_test.go, the Dependencies + deps.CaptureHandler wiring from capture_test.go) plus a recordingPipeline Pipeliner stub that records the ctx handed to Process. The test cancels the request context before CaptureHandler runs and asserts Process received a non-cancelled ctx + the prompt text preserved. RED captured (durable capture write ran with a cancelled context (err=context canceled), FAIL internal/api, exit 1). Applied the fix in internal/api/capture.go: added the "context" import (capture.go did not import context) and swapped d.Pipeline.Process(r.Context(), ...) -> d.Pipeline.Process(context.WithoutCancel(r.Context()), ...) with an explanatory comment. GREEN captured (PASS, ok internal/api 0.230s, exit 0). Change confined to capture.go (import + the durable Process call + comment) + the new test file; response-path d.DB.Healthy(r.Context()) / writeJSON / writeError left request-scoped. Go 1.25.10 supports context.WithoutCancel.

Focused unit RED->GREEN complete via the repo CLI (./smackerel.sh test unit --go --go-run ... --verbose, Dockerized go test -run ... ./...): RED exit 1 (context canceled) -> GREEN exit 0 (PASS). Handler-family regression (./smackerel.sh test unit --go --go-run 'TestCaptureHandler' --verbose): every TestCaptureHandler_* in internal/api PASSES (ok internal/api 0.086s, exit 0) — no collateral regression in the touched handler. PENDING: the shared live-stack E2E disconnect-race regression covering BOTH /api/assistant/turn (BUG-069-002) and /api/capture (this bug) against a real pipeline.Processor (Postgres + NATS) requires `./smackerel.sh up` — not runnable in this session; routed to bubbles.test -> bubbles.validate for done-ceiling certification. Full integration/e2e/chaos/stress sweep is out of scope for this focused fix.

Delta = capture.go (+"context" import, 1 durable Process call line swapped to context.WithoutCancel, + explanatory comment) + new test file. internal/api package green under the TestCaptureHandler selector (ok internal/api 0.086s, exit 0); the GREEN focused run also compiled the entire module (go test ./...) with exit 0 (no build break anywhere). No behavioral change to the response path (DB health re-check / writeJSON / writeError stay request-scoped), to non-capture handlers, or to the Telegram path. No tests deleted/skipped/weakened. REGRESSION_FREE for the touched surface (unit scope).

Security/compliance audit of the changed surface. Exactly one durable Process call site in internal/api/capture.go (line 110) now uses context.WithoutCancel (grep -nc context.WithoutCancel = 1) — durable-write-once invariant unchanged (BS-001). The response-path DB health re-check (d.DB.Healthy(r.Context())) and writeJSON/writeError stay request-scoped, so the 4xx/5xx no-internals-leak behavior is unchanged. context.WithoutCancel preserves parent request-scoped Values only (request id / trace via middleware.GetReqID, consumed by submitForProcessing for the artifact TraceID); no auth/session identity leakage (the capture path uses explicit request fields + SourceID, not ctx-sourced identity). No new secret/PII/financial-action surface (P10 boundary intact). No NO-DEFAULTS violation (no new config). OWASP A01/A03/A05/A07: no findings on the changed code. Verdict: SECURE.

Reviewed the change through the simplify lens (read internal/api/capture.go ~L93-110 + capture_disconnect_test.go). The fix is a single durable Process call-site swap to context.WithoutCancel plus the previously-absent context import, an explanatory comment, and one adversarial test — no new abstraction, branch, indirection, or config. Nothing to simplify: the one-line cancellation decoupling is already the minimal correct form.

Reviewed through the stability lens. The fix strictly improves durability — the /api/capture durable write no longer aborts on client disconnect. context.WithoutCancel only severs cancellation propagation while preserving request-scoped Values; no new goroutine/lock/retry/timeout, and the durable Process is still invoked exactly once. No leak or stability regression introduced (internal/api package stays green; the GREEN focused run compiled the whole module with exit 0).

Reviewed through the security lens. context.WithoutCancel preserves only the parent's request-scoped Values (request id / trace), not auth/session identity — the capture path uses explicit request fields + SourceID, not ctx-sourced identity. No new input surface, no secret/PII exposure; the response-path writeJSON/writeError no-internals-leak behavior is unchanged. OWASP A01/A03/A05/A07: no findings on the changed line. Corroborates the prior audit phase (SECURE).

Reviewed through the validation lens. The adversarial unit RED->GREEN (TestCaptureHandler_CaptureSurvivesClientDisconnect; RED exit 1 context canceled -> GREEN exit 0) plus the SHARED live-stack durable regression tests/integration/capture_disconnect_durability_test.go::TestCaptureDisconnectDurability_ProcessorSurvivesClientCancel (real pipeline.Processor + Postgres + NATS; ARTIFACTS LastSeq 0->1; adversarial raw-cancel persists 0 rows; captured 2026-06-30) ARE the validation and prove the fix is load-bearing at BOTH the handler wiring and the shared durable processor path. Scope-1 DoD fully satisfied. A durability correctness fix is not an SLA/stress scope (Gate G026). Status held at in_progress for the orchestrator's commit-then-certify; the certification.status flip is validate/orchestrator-owned, NOT self-certified here.

executionHistory.summary: bubbles.code-review MVP/evo-x2 readiness sweep (F-01) discovered F-069-CR-CAPTURE-ENDPOINT-CTX-CANCEL: the direct POST /api/capture handler binds the durable pipeline.Process write to r.Context(), silently dropping the capture on client disconnect — the same root cause as the already-fixed BUG-069-002, at the endpoint BUG-069-002 never covered. LANDED the fix: internal/api/capture.go now calls d.Pipeline.Process(context.WithoutCancel(r.Context()), ...) (+ added the missing context import), proven by TestCaptureHandler_CaptureSurvivesClientDisconnect adversarial unit regression (RED->GREEN) + handler-family regression green (ok internal/api). ROUTED the remaining done-ceiling certification to bubbles.test -> bubbles.validate as ONE SHARED live-stack E2E disconnect-race regression covering BOTH /api/assistant/turn (BUG-069-002) and /api/capture (this bug); not runnable in this session (no live stack). The already-done parent spec 069 artifacts and the BUG-069-002 packet are left untouched; this finding and fix are recorded entirely in this bug packet.
